scientific_question: Does physically constrained daily intervention timing add canonical ReT beyond constants, weekly open-loop timing, and weekly clairvoyant timing under thesis-authorized recurrent-risk regimes?

secondary_temporal_panel.recovery_definition: service >= 95 percent of the pre-event median and overdue backlog <= 105 percent of its pre-event median for seven consecutive daily observations

forbidden: select a policy on a temporal secondary

risk_regimes.impact_sensitivity_rule: predeclared report-only sensitivities; they cannot rescue a failed primary frequency regime
risk_regimes.profile_selection_rule: use current as the comparator, then select the first passing augmented regime in order R24_increased, R22_increased, R22_R24_increased; never select on timing outcomes; if none pass, stop before timing execution

posture_frontier.family: Track-A common I1344 buffer fraction plus S1/S2/S3
posture_frontier.low_posture: best safe constant in current regime at frozen cap
posture_frontier.high_posture: best safe constant in selected increased regime at frozen cap

physical_intervention.hold: preserve requested posture and create no buffer top-up or exogenous RNG call
physical_intervention.dispatch: daily immediate only when the selected Track-A posture changes a dispatch field; otherwise thesis baseline
physical_intervention.shifts: one-level-per-day upward ramp with the existing surge-hour budget; HOLD preserves the request while physical ramping may continue
physical_intervention.buffers: 168-hour immutable commitment; no cancellation or overwrite; at most one new commitment per 168 hours
physical_intervention.churn_cost: none; report intervention count and change magnitude instead

restricted_privileged_oracle.future_information: realized R22 and R24 event onset/end times only
restricted_privileged_oracle.conflict_rule: overlapping high-posture windows are unioned; same-time events are applied before the daily observation and decision

comparators.open_loop: all 256 eight-week binary low/high calendars repeated periodically
comparators.weekly_clairvoyant: same restricted event windows rounded down to the preceding 168-hour decision boundary
comparators.observable: EWMA plus hysteresis trigger using realized past risk, backlog age and inventory shortfall only

exclude_if_worse: realized shift, surge, transport, or buffer resources

promotion_gate.next_if_pass: freeze one observable classical controller and a new OOS validation contract
promotion_gate.next_if_fail: close this timing family without changing metrics, offsets, impacts, postures, or cadence
promotion_gate.secondary_only_win: may support a recovery-rapidity analysis only; cannot open observable validation, a learner, or Paper 2
promotion_gate.observable_conversion_report: report development eta_timing = observable EWMA increment over the best nonobservable comparator divided by restricted privileged increment over that same comparator; this is descriptive on burned tapes and is not confirmed H_obs
